message.reasoning:
We need to output a JSON response matching the response contract. The task: Identify exact files and invariants involved in turn-trace validation before editing replay or persistence behavior. Class: prepare_to_edit. Allowed tools: read_file, list_dir, search. Verification Targets: command: cargo test turn_trace_validation_rejects_unvalidated_responses.

We need to gather evidence. We have already done search and read_file actions. Now we need to produce a JSON output.

The response contract: JSON only with shape {"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}.

We need to decide kind. Probably we are ready to edit? We have identified the relevant files. We have done search for schema version definitions and read_file of artifacts.rs. We can consider we have evidence. readiness_state likely "ready_to_edit". So kind="complete"? Actually response contract fields include "kind":"think|tool_call|complete". If we are done, use "complete". Then assistant_message brief status. tool_call may be null (omit). acquired_fact_ids list of fact ids we have acquired. We need to provide some fact ids. What fact ids are relevant? Possibly from evidence spec. Could use IDs from earlier evidence spec evidence-03.evidence.json line 41 had command "cargo test turn_trace_validation_rejects_unvalidated_responses". Could treat that as a fact id: maybe "evidence-03". So acquired_fact_ids could be ["evidence-03"].

We need also to include tool_call if kind is tool_call. Since we are done, use "complete" with no tool_call field.

The JSON must be valid and no extra fields. The structure: {"kind":"complete","assistant_message":"..."} plus optional tool_call and acquired_fact_ids and readiness_state. Actually contract: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","prompt_version":"v1","model_slug":"<provider model id>","provider":"<provider slug>","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call": {"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"} . Wait includes prompt_version, model_slug, provider etc? That seems from earlier spec for tool call. But in our case we are outputting final answer, not tool call. According to earlier instruction: "Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}". It didn't mention prompt_version etc now; maybe that was earlier spec for tool_call. Now they say "Response Contract" includes shape: { "kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>", "tool_call": {...}, "acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"], "readiness_state":"..." }. But they also earlier said "Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","prompt_version\\\":\\\"v1\\\",\\\"model_slug\\\":\\\"<provider model id>\\\",\\\"provider\\\":\\\"<provider slug>\\\",\\\"assistant_message\\\":\\\"<brief status>\\\",\\\"tool_call\\\":{{"tool_name\\\":\"<one registered tool name>\\\",\"payload\":{{}}}}, "acquired_fact_ids\\":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state\\":\\\"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit\\\"}}" . That includes many fields. However later they simplified: "Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}" . So we need to include kind, assistant_message, tool_call (if any), acquired_fact_ids, readiness_state. For kind "complete", we likely omit tool_call (or can set null?). The spec says tool_call must be omitted unless kind is tool_call. So we can omit tool_call entirely. Then JSON includes kind, assistant_message, acquired_fact_ids, readiness_state. No extra fields.

Thus output something like:

{
  "kind":"complete",
  "assistant_message":"Identified schema version constants in crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs",
  "acquired_fact_ids":["evidence-03"],
  "readiness_state":"ready_to_edit"
}

But need to be careful about quoting and JSON validity. Use double quotes. Ensure no trailing commas.

Thus final answer.
